title: The basics
assignment: Go over the concepts from this section. If something is unclear, go back to the lesson.
groups.0.title: Tags and elements
groups.0.items.0.def:
opens.

closes. The slash goes BEFORE the name and means "this is where it ends". Not after it —

closes nothing.
groups.0.items.1.def: Opening tag + content + closing tag. All three together.
groups.0.items.2.def:
The text sits INSIDE the tag, not after it.

Alex

, not

Alex.
groups.0.items.3.def: Forget the closing tag and the element never ends — it eats everything after it. The page shows, there is no error, and it is still wrong. When something is inexplicably huge, look for an unclosed tag first.
groups.0.items.4.def: A tag inside a tag. Whatever opened last closes first. Cross them over and the browser shows no error — it guesses what you meant.
groups.1.title: The tags you know
groups.1.items.0.def: First-level heading. It does not mean "big" — it means "this is the page's heading". The size is the browser's decision.
groups.1.items.1.def: Paragraph. Each paragraph gets its own tag. Without it, sentences stick together on one line no matter how many times you press Enter.
groups.2.title: What you are actually doing
groups.2.items.0.term: You describe, you do not draw
groups.2.items.0.def:
You never say how big, which font, which color. You only say what a thing IS. The same words in

and in

are one sentence with two different roles.
groups.2.items.1.def: The page looks styled because the browser loads CSS of its own before it sees yours. Big headings, margins, list bullets and blue links come from there. Your CSS overrides it.
groups.2.items.2.def: The person with a screen reader. Readers have shortcuts to jump paragraph to paragraph and heading to heading. Bare text has nothing to jump to.
groups.3.title: Three traps
groups.3.items.0.def: Any run of spaces, tabs and newlines becomes ONE space. That is why you cannot align with spaces — and why indentation in code is free.
groups.3.items.1.term: An empty paragraph is not spacing
groups.3.items.1.def: The screen reader announces a paragraph and reads nothing. Spacing is a look, and looks are made with CSS.
groups.3.items.2.def: HTML says what exists. CSS says how it looks. JavaScript says what it does. In that order — you cannot color something that is not there.
